attack_scenario.title: Reentrancy attack on withdraw function
attack_scenario.description: Attacker can recursively call withdraw before balance update to drain contract
preconditions: Attacker has non-zero balance in contract
Attacker controls receiver contract with callback

attack_steps.action: Deploy malicious contract with fallback function
Fallback function triggers recursive withdraw call
Repeat until contract drained or gas exhausted

attack_scenario.poc_sketch: contract Attack { receive() external payable { if (address(vault).balance > 0) vault.withdraw(); } }

evidence.description: External value transfer before state update
No reentrancy protection on payable function

graph_queries.query: FIND functions WHERE visibility IN [public, external] AND TRANSFERS_VALUE_OUT = true
FIND functions WHERE operation_sequence CONTAINS [TRANSFERS_VALUE_OUT, WRITES_USER_BALANCE]

assumptions: Contract balance exceeds attacker's initial deposit
Gas limit allows multiple recursive calls
No external reentrancy guard in protocol
limitations: Cannot verify external contract behavior without full source
Actual gas cost may vary with state size
